model: ProofCast editorial (composed from TxLINE-verified events + web2 names)
text:
Once upon a matchday — it was the quarter-final, on the 9th of July 2026 — two teams walked out under the soft evening lights. France booked their semi-final place with a composed, professional win over Morocco. Morocco, the tournament's great disruptors, ran into a France side finding its rhythm at exactly the right time.

In the 60th minute, Kylian Mbappé sent the ball gently home, and the score whispered 1–0. In the 66th minute, Ousmane Dembélé sent the ball gently home, and the score whispered 2–0.

Kylian Mbappé missed a first-half penalty, and for a while it looked as though Morocco might make him pay. He made amends after the break, sweeping in his 20th career World Cup goal and then turning provider. Ousmane Dembélé's fifth of the tournament settled matters and sent Les Bleus into the last four. France did not need to be spectacular; they simply needed to be efficient, and they were.

The crowd hummed like a distant sea, rising and falling with every chance.

Somewhere in the stands, a small supporter watched with very wide eyes.

And so the story ended France 2–0 Morocco. France carried on their long journey, and Morocco lay down to rest. Every goal in this tale is a true one — kept safe and provable on the blockchain by TxLINE — so you can close your eyes knowing not a single fact was ever made up. Sleep well, and goodnight.
